LLM auditresponse for sublime-text-2.sh:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The script `sublime-text-2.sh` is a wrapper intended to launch Sublime Text 2 with appropriate environment settings and command-line arguments. It sets `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` to include a local library directory (`/opt/sublime-text-2/lib`), which is a common practice for bundled applications that rely on older or custom libraries (e.g., an older `libpng`). While modifying `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` can be a security risk if it enables library preloading attacks, in this context it is used predictably and locally, only for the application's own libraries.

The script also constructs arguments for the `.desktop` launcher, adding `--class=sublime_text` and `--project` when a `.sublime-project` file is passed. It uses standard shell conditionals and string manipulation, with no obfuscation, external downloads, or suspicious system calls. The use of `exec` to run the binary and background it with `&` is consistent with GUI application launchers. There is no data exfiltration, unexpected network activity, or execution of external scripts.

All operations are confined to the application's directory and standard launch behavior. The code aligns with normal packaging practices for self-contained GUI applications on Linux.
</details>
<summary>
Normal wrapper script with harmless LD_LIBRARY_PATH usage
</summary>
</security_assessment>
